prevalence.value: common; highest in children <5 years (viral gastroenteritis dominant)
prevalence.source: WHO Europe / European pediatric gastroenteritis epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; low mortality with high ORS access

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; high healthcare utilization during winter viral peaks

vulnerablePopulations: children with chronic disease or immunosuppression
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with dehydration assessment; stool testing reserved for severe/bloody/prolonged illness or outbreak investigation

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with ORS and prompt care; hospitalization in select cases

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and ED pathways emphasize dehydration assessment and safe discharge criteria
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ORS and continued feeding; IV fluids for severe dehydration; antibiotic stewardship is strong
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Seasonal hospital utilization peaks and outbreak control (norovirus)
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High access to ORS and robust pediatric emergency care
